fields.description:
## What
The `rebar metrics` subcommand: parses `--since/--until` and optional `--bucket day|week|month`, renders the full registry over the range as JSON (default) or a text summary, and is the single entry point wiring the readers.

## Files
- `src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py` ‚Äî new: arg parsing + render loop over `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py`.
- `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` ‚Äî register the `metrics` read subcommand in the command-set frozensets and dispatch.
- `src/rebar/_cli/help/metrics.txt` ‚Äî new help text.
- `src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt` ‚Äî add the `metrics` line to the subcommand overview.
- `tests/golden/cli_help/*.out` ‚Äî regenerate the overview snapshots (`__overview_help.out`, `__overview_dashhelp.out`) and add `metrics__help.out`, since the subcommand set is enumerated there.
- `docs/cli-reference.md` ‚Äî document the command.
- `tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py` ‚Äî new CLI subprocess test.

## Approach
A thin CLI over the registry keeps the command complete-once: it builds a **context object** and calls `rebar.metrics.registry.evaluate(spec, context)` for every `MetricSpec`, emitting value-or-`unavailable` per metric, so new signals appear with no CLI change.

**The context object (the contract the readers already expect):** every reader's registered `compute` is a single-arg callable that reads `ctx.repo_root`, `ctx.since`, `ctx.until` (via `getattr(ctx, "repo_root", None)` etc. ‚Äî see the git/event/sidecar readers). So `metrics.py` constructs a small context (a dataclass or `SimpleNamespace`) carrying exactly `repo_root` (the resolved store root), `since`, and `until` (the parsed range), and passes it to `evaluate`. For `--bucket`, it builds one context per bucket sub-range and evaluates the registry per bucket, keying the output by bucket.

**Adapter isolation (portability guard from 1f77/538c):** `metrics.py` reaches metrics only through `rebar.metrics.registry` (and the package import that populates `REGISTRY`); it must NOT import any `rebar.metrics.adapters` module. Because that adapters package may not exist yet at this point in the stack, the guard is a **source-level scan**: the test reads `src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py` and asserts its source contains no `rebar.metrics.adapters` import (a text/AST check that holds regardless of whether the package exists). Adapter-backed metrics reach the command only through `REGISTRY`, never a direct import ‚Äî so the core command stays harness-agnostic.

Registering a subcommand touches the enumerated help/golden surfaces (`overview.txt` + the `tests/golden/cli_help` snapshots) and the generated `docs/cli-reference.md`, all part of the edit set. Read-only; `--bucket day|week|month` splits the `[since, until]` range into sub-ranges and evaluates the registry once per sub-range (each reader already accepts `since`/`until` as ISO date bounds ‚Äî the command does not reach into HLC timestamps itself; the readers do their own timestamp filtering).

**Output formats.** JSON (default) is the machine contract: `{"since","until","metrics": {<id>: {"lens","source","confidence","value"} | {"unavailable": {"reason","accruing_since"}}, ...}}`. `--output text` is a deterministic human summary ‚Äî one line per metric in registry order: `<id>  [<lens>/<source>]  <value-or-"unavailable: <reason>">` (a fixed-width, self-defined layout ‚Äî NOT borrowed from `rebar audit`, whose schema is structurally different); an `unavailable` metric renders as `unavailable: <reason>`. Both formats include every registered metric id.

## Tests
- CLI (real subprocess against a temp store): `rebar metrics --since ... --until ... --output json` exits 0 and emits a `metrics` map containing every registered id; an unpopulated metric shows `unavailable`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py` passes: `rebar metrics --since 2026-01-01 --until 2026-07-01 --output json` exits 0 and the emitted `.metrics` map contains an entry for EVERY id in `rebar.metrics.registry.REGISTRY` (the test asserts `set(output["metrics"].keys()) >= {s.id for s in REGISTRY}` ‚Äî NOT merely `length > 0`), each entry being either a value (with `source`+`confidence`) or an `unavailable` object.
- [ ] the portability guard holds: a test asserts `src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py` does not import `rebar.metrics.adapters` (the core command stays harness-agnostic; adapter-backed metrics arrive only via `REGISTRY`).
- [ ] `rebar metrics --help` renders the help file and the command is listed by bare `rebar` ‚Äî CLI test asserts exit 0 and help contains `--since`.
- [ ] the golden CLI-help snapshots are regenerated and the `tests/golden/cli_help` suite passes with `metrics` listed.
- [ ] `--bucket week` yields a per-bucket series for a metric with history ‚Äî CLI test asserts the JSON contains at least one bucket key.
